summary: chain-chai-await fetches and evaluates remote JavaScript from an anonymous paste host
details: The package impersonates the pino logger (README badge, keywords, module.exports.pino alias, and lib/ filenames such as proto.js, redaction.js, transport.js, multistream.js, worker.js all mirror pino) but its main entry point exports a middleware factory that, when invoked, spawns a detached Node child process running lib/caller.js. caller.js HTTPS-GETs https://jsonkeeper.com/b/EXSIF and passes the response body to Function.constructor(...) for execution, with retry logic. lib/const.js stores a second loader URL (https://jsonkeeper.com/b/ZK45J) and a custom header name base64-encoded to hide them from casual review. jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, mutable paste host — the author can change the served JavaScript at any time, giving them unbounded remote code execution on any host that loads this package. The detached child + stdio:'ignore' pattern hides the loader's execution from the parent process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
